2155	// populated in the JSON output.

2158			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",

2160		}

2167		}}

2169

2171

2176		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
2177		assert.False(t, res.StartedAt.IsZero())

2183	// TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough locks in the resolution contract for
2184	// execute-bead's model option: the value supplied via ExecuteBeadOptions.Model
2185	// is passed verbatim to the runner, and an empty value is not silently replaced
2186	// by any hardcoded or catalog-derived default. This regression test guards
2187	// against routing layers injecting a model (e.g. a stale vendor/model like
2188	// "z-ai/glm-5.1") when the caller did not request one — the case the agent
2189	// harness resolves from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml must be preserved by
2190	// ExecuteBead handing the runner an empty Model so the harness's own
2191	// resolution chain runs.
2192	func TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough(t *testing.T) {
2193		t.Run("empty model stays empty through ExecuteBead", func(t *testing.T) {
2194			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2195				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2196				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2197			}
2198			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2199			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2200	
2201			// No --model flag supplied to execute-bead.
2202			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
2203	
2204			assert.Equal(t, "", runner.last.Model,
2205				"runner must receive an empty Model when no --model flag is provided; "+
2206					"any non-empty value here indicates a routing layer injected a default, "+
2207					"which would override the harness's own config-driven resolution")
2208		})
2209	
2210		t.Run("explicit model is forwarded verbatim", func(t *testing.T) {
2211			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2212				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2213				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2214			}
2215			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2216			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2217	
2218			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--model", "qwen3.5-27b")
2219	
2220			assert.Equal(t, "qwen3.5-27b", runner.last.Model,
2221				"runner must receive the exact --model value the caller passed")
2222		})
2223	}

2225	func TestExecuteBeadStatusMapping(t *testing.T) {
2226		cases := []struct {
2227			name     string
2228			result   agent.ExecuteBeadResult
2229			expected string
2230		}{
2231			{
2232				name:     "merged success",
2233				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "merged", ExitCode: 0},
2234				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
2235			},
2236			{
2237				name:     "no changes stays non-success",
2238				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "no-changes", ExitCode: 0},
2239				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
2240			},
2241			{
2242				name:     "execution failure dominates preserved outcome",
2243				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "preserved", ExitCode: 1, Reason: "agent execution failed"},
2244				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
2245			},
2246			{
2247				name:     "error outcome stays execution failure",
2248				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "error", ExitCode: -1, Reason: "timeout after 5m"},
2249				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
2250			},
2251			{
2252				name:     "land conflict",
2253				result:   agent.ExecuteBeadResult{Outcome: "preserved", ExitCode: 0, Reason: "merge failed"},
2254				expected: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict,
2255			},
2256		}
2257	
2258		for _, tc := range cases {
2259			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
2260				res := tc.result
2261				res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
2262				res.Detail = agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
2263				assert.Equal(t, tc.expected, res.Status)
2264				assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Detail)
2265			})
2266		}
2267	}

2269	// seedGateDocs writes a governing spec doc and a required execution gate doc
2270	// into workDir/docs/ so they are copied into worktrees by fakeExecuteBeadGit.
2271	func seedGateDocs(t *testing.T, workDir string, gateCommand []string) {
2272		t.Helper()
2273		docsDir := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs")
2274		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(docsDir, 0o755))
2275	
2276		specDoc := "---\nddx:\n  id: FEAT-GATE-TEST\n---\n# Spec: Gate Test\n"
2277		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(docsDir, "spec-gate-test.md"), []byte(specDoc), 0o644))
2278	
2279		var cmdYAML string
2280		for _, part := range gateCommand {
2281			cmdYAML += fmt.Sprintf("      - \"%s\"\n", part)
2282		}
2283		gateDoc := fmt.Sprintf("---\nddx:\n  id: EXEC-GATE-TEST\n  depends_on:\n    - FEAT-GATE-TEST\n  execution:\n    kind: command\n    required: true\n    command:\n%s---\n# Gate\n", cmdYAML)
2284		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(docsDir, "exec-gate-test.md"), []byte(gateDoc), 0o644))
2285	}

2287	// TestExecuteBeadGatePass verifies that execute-bead evaluates required gates
2288	// against an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev and merges when all gates pass.
2289	// Wired by ddx-14c0e790 (interactive execute-bead gate eval).
2290	func TestExecuteBeadGatePass(t *testing.T) {
2291		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2292			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2293			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2294		}
2295		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
2296		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2297	
2298		seedExecuteBead(t, f.WorkingDir, &bead.Bead{
2299			ID:        "gate-bead",
2300			Title:     "Bead with required gate",
2301			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
2302			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
2303			Extra:     map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-GATE-TEST"},
2304		})
2305		seedGateDocs(t, f.WorkingDir, []string{"true"})
2306	
2307		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "gate-bead")
2308	
2309		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
2310		require.Len(t, res.GateResults, 1, "required gate must be evaluated")
2311		assert.Equal(t, "pass", res.GateResults[0].Status)
2312	}

200		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git

206	// fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit returns a LandingAdvancer callback that maps the
207	// fake git's Merge/UpdateRef semantics onto the coordinator-pattern advancer
208	// interface. Semantically, "mergeCalls" now means "number of times
209	// LandBeadResult invoked the advancer (attempted to advance the target
210	// branch)". Used by tests that were written against the old Merge() path so
211	// they continue to pass after the land-coordinator refactor.
212	func fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git *fakeExecuteBeadGit) func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
213		return func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
214			if err := git.Merge("", res.ResultRev); err != nil {
215				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
216				// Record the preserve ref in the fake's refs map so tests that
217				// assert git.refs[preserveRef] continue to pass.
218				_ = git.UpdateRef("", preserveRef, res.ResultRev)
219				return &agent.LandResult{
220					Status:      "preserved",
221					PreserveRef: preserveRef,
222					Reason:      "merge failed",
223				}, nil
224			}
225			return &agent.LandResult{Status: "landed", NewTip: res.ResultRev}, nil
226		}
227	}
228	
229	func assertPreserveRef(t *testing.T, ref, beadID, baseRev string) {
230		t.Helper()
231		shortSHA := baseRev
232		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
233			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
234		}
235		pattern := fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/%s/\d{8}T\d{6}Z-%s$`,
236			regexp.QuoteMeta(beadID), regexp.QuoteMeta(shortSHA))
237		require.Regexp(t, pattern, ref)
238	}

240	// runExecuteBead invokes the execute-bead command through the cobra tree and returns
241	// the parsed JSON result. It extracts the JSON object from the combined output,
242	// skipping any leading note/warning lines written to stderr.
243	func runExecuteBead(t *testing.T, f *CommandFactory, git *fakeExecuteBeadGit, beadID string, extraArgs ...string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
244		t.Helper()
245		root := f.NewRootCommand()
246		args := append([]string{"agent", "execute-bead", beadID, "--json"}, extraArgs...)
247		out, err := executeCommand(root, args...)
248		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
249		return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
250	}
251	
252	func parseExecuteBeadJSON(t *testing.T, out string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
253		t.Helper()
254		// Strip any non-JSON prefix lines (e.g. stderr notes written to the shared buffer).
255		jsonStart := strings.Index(out, "{")
256		require.NotEqual(t, -1, jsonStart, "output should contain JSON: %s", out)
257		jsonPart := out[jsonStart:]
258		var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
259		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(jsonPart))
260		require.NoError(t, dec.Decode(&res), "output should be valid JSON: %s", jsonPart)
261		return res
262	}

269			return

292	func copyTree(src, dst string) error {
293		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
294			if err != nil {
295				return err
296			}
297			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
298			if err != nil {
299				return err
300			}
301			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
302			if info.IsDir() {
303				return os.MkdirAll(target, info.Mode())
304			}
305			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755); err != nil {
306				return err
307			}
308			in, err := os.Open(path)
309			if err != nil {
310				return err
311			}
312			defer in.Close()
313			out, err := os.Create(target)
314			if err != nil {
315				return err
316			}
317			if _, err := io.Copy(out, in); err != nil {
318				out.Close()
319				return err
320			}
321			if err := out.Close(); err != nil {
322				return err
323			}
324			return os.Chmod(target, info.Mode())
325		})
326	}
327	
328	func copyTestFile(src, dst string) error {
329		info, err := os.Stat(src)
330		if err != nil {
331			return err
332		}
333		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o755); err != nil {
334			return err
335		}
336		in, err := os.Open(src)
337		if err != nil {
338			return err
339		}
340		defer in.Close()
341		out, err := os.Create(dst)
342		if err != nil {
343			return err
344		}
345		if _, err := io.Copy(out, in); err != nil {
346			out.Close()
347			return err
348		}
349		if err := out.Close(); err != nil {
350			return err
351		}
352		return os.Chmod(dst, info.Mode())
353	}

355	// TestExecuteBeadMerge verifies that when the agent produces a real commit and
356	// the target branch can advance, the outcome is "merged". Exercises real git
357	// plumbing (worktree add/remove, ref update, file sync) and the script harness
358	// driving an actual commit — no fakes for the components under test.
359	func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
360		workDir := t.TempDir()
361	
362		scrubEnv := func() []string {
363			parent := os.Environ()
364			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
365			for _, kv := range parent {
366				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
367					continue
368				}
369				env = append(env, kv)
370			}
371			return env
372		}
373		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
374			t.Helper()
375			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
376			cmd.Dir = workDir
377			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
378			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
379			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
380			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
381		}
382	
383		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
384		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
385		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
386		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
387		runGit("add", ".")
388		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
389	
390		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
391			ID:        "my-bead",
392			Title:     "Test execute-bead merge",
393			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
394			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
395		})
396		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
397		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
398		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
399	
400		// Directive file drives the script harness to make one real commit in the
401		// worker worktree, replacing fakeAgentRunner's canned Result struct.
402		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
403		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
404			"create-file hello.txt hello-content\n"+
405				"commit feat: add hello\n",
406		), 0o644))
407	
408		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
409		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
410		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
411	
412		root := f.NewRootCommand()
413		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
414			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
415		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
416		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
417	
418		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
419		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
420		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
421		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
422		assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)
423		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
424		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
425		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
426	
427		// Real-git assertion: main must have advanced to ResultRev via Land().
428		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD"),
429			"main should advance to ResultRev after a successful merge")
430	
431		// The worker worktree must have been created and cleaned up: no entries
432		// under the execute-bead prefix remain in `git worktree list`.
433		wtList := runGit("worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
434		assert.NotContains(t, wtList, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-",
435			"execute-bead worktree should be removed after the run")
436	
437		// File from the agent's commit must be materialized on main through real
438		// merge/ff semantics (regression guard for ddx-eaebaffb: update-ref
439		// bypassing the working tree).
440		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"))
441		require.NoError(t, err, "hello.txt should materialize on main after Land()")
442		assert.Equal(t, "hello-content", string(content))
443	}
444	
445	// TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure verifies that when merge fails
446	// the result is preserved under a hidden ref. Exercises real git plumbing
447	// and the script harness producing an actual conflicting commit — no fakes
448	// for the components under test.
449	func TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure(t *testing.T) {
450		workDir := t.TempDir()
451	
452		scrubEnv := func() []string {
453			parent := os.Environ()
454			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
455			for _, kv := range parent {
456				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
457					continue
458				}
459				env = append(env, kv)
460			}
461			return env
462		}
463		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
464			t.Helper()
465			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
466			cmd.Dir = workDir
467			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
468			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
469			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
470			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
471		}
472	
473		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
474		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
475		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
476		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
477		runGit("add", ".")
478		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
479	
480		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
481			ID:        "my-bead",
482			Title:     "Test execute-bead preserve on merge failure",
483			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
484			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
485		})
486		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
487		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
488		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
489	
490		// Advance main past baseSHA with a sibling commit that writes hello.txt
491		// with content different from what the agent will produce. Once the
492		// agent commits its own hello.txt on a worktree rooted at baseSHA,
493		// Land() must attempt a merge and hit a content conflict, triggering
494		// the preserve path.
495		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"), []byte("sibling-content\n"), 0o644))
496		runGit("add", "hello.txt")
497		runGit("commit", "-m", "feat: sibling conflicting change")
498		siblingSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
499

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"testing"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	)
17	
18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}
59	
60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	// Repo setup
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}
109	
110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	// Directive file helper
112	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	// writeDirectiveFile writes a directive file at path with the given lines.
115	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {
116		t.Helper()
117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755))
118		content := strings.Join(lines, "\n") + "\n"
119		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644))
120	}
121	
122	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
230	// Git assertion helpers
231	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	
233	// gitCommitCount returns the number of commits reachable from ref (plus any
234	// additional git rev-list args like "--not", "SHA").
235	func gitCommitCount(t *testing.T, projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) int {
236		t.Helper()
237		args := append([]string{"rev-list", "--count"}, refAndArgs...)
238		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, args...)
239		n, err := strconv.Atoi(out)
240		require.NoError(t, err, "git rev-list --count %v", refAndArgs)
241		return n
242	}
243	
244	// gitHasMergeCommits returns true when any merge commits exist in ref's history.
245	func gitHasMergeCommits(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
246		t.Helper()
247		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--merges", "--format=%H", ref)
248		return out != ""
249	}
250	
251	// refExists returns true when the given git ref is present in the repo.
252	func refExists(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
253		t.Helper()
254		_, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref)
255		return err == nil
256	}
257	
258	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	// Store helpers
260	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	// makeLoopStore creates an ExecuteBeadLoopStore backed by a bead.Store rooted
263	// in ddxDir. The store is already initialised by newScriptHarnessRepo.
264	func makeLoopStore(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) ExecuteBeadLoopStore {
265		t.Helper()
266		return bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
267	}
268	
269	// countClosedBeads counts how many beads in ddxDir have status "closed".
270	func countClosedBeads(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) int {
271		t.Helper()
272		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
273		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
274		require.NoError(t, err)
275		n := 0
276		for _, b := range all {
277			if b.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
278				n++
279			}
280		}
281		return n
282	}
283	
284	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	// Shell quoting helper
286	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	// escapeForShell escapes a string for embedding in a single-quoted shell argument.
289	func escapeForShell(s string) string {
290		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, "'", `'\''`)
291	}

1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land_test.go — Land() coordinator-pattern unit tests.
4	//
5	// These tests run against a real temp git repo so they exercise the actual
6	// git plumbing (update-ref, merge --no-ff, worktree add, etc.) rather than a
7	// mock. Each scenario asserts that the target tip advances correctly and —
8	// crucially for replay fidelity — that the worker's own commit is never
9	// rewritten. Its parent always stays BaseRev so replay sees the same inputs
10	// the worker saw at execution time.
11	
12	import (
13		"fmt"
14		"os"
15		"os/exec"
16		"path/filepath"
17		"strings"
18		"sync"
19		"testing"
20		"time"
21	)
22	
23	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Test helpers (real git repo fixtures)
25	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	type landTestRepo struct {
28		t       *testing.T
29		dir     string
30		origin  string // path to a bare origin repo, or "" if no remote
31		branch  string // target branch
32		baseSHA string // the initial commit on the target branch
33	}
34	
35	func newLandTestRepo(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
36		t.Helper()
37		dir := t.TempDir()
38		r := &landTestRepo{t: t, dir: dir, branch: "main"}
39		r.runGit("init", "-b", "main")
40		r.runGit("config", "user.name", "Test")
41		r.runGit("config", "user.email", "test@test.local")
42		r.writeFile("README.md", "# test\n")
43		r.runGit("add", "-A")
44		r.runGit("commit", "-m", "init")
45		r.baseSHA = r.resolveRef("refs/heads/main")
46		return r
47	}
48	
49	// newLandTestRepoWithOrigin creates a test repo whose origin is a separate
50	// bare repo. Used by the push-ff-only test.
51	func newLandTestRepoWithOrigin(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
52		t.Helper()
53		r := newLandTestRepo(t)
54	
55		bareDir := t.TempDir()
56		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", "--bare", "-b", "main", bareDir)
57		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("git init --bare: %s: %v", string(out), err)
59		}
60		r.origin = bareDir
61		r.runGit("remote", "add", "origin", bareDir)
62		r.runGit("push", "-u", "origin", "main")
63		return r
64	}
65	
66	func (r *landTestRepo) runGit(args ...string) string {
67		r.t.Helper()
68		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", r.dir}, args...)...)
69		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
70		if err != nil {
71			r.t.Fatalf("git %s: %s: %v", strings.Join(args, " "), string(out), err)
72		}
73		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
74	}
75	
76	func (r *landTestRepo) writeFile(path, content string) {
77		r.t.Helper()
78		full := filepath.Join(r.dir, path)
79		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(full), 0o755); err != nil {
80			r.t.Fatal(err)
81		}
82		if err := os.WriteFile(full, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
83			r.t.Fatal(err)
84		}
85	}
86	
87	func (r *landTestRepo) resolveRef(ref string) string {
88		r.t.Helper()
89		return r.runGit("rev-parse", ref)
90	}
91	
92	// commitOn creates a detached-head commit at baseSHA in a throwaway worktree
93	// and returns the new commit SHA. The commit is reachable via object store
94	// but not via any branch in the main repo, simulating what a worker worktree
95	// produces after ExecuteBead cleans up.
96	func (r *landTestRepo) commitOn(baseSHA, path, content, msg string) string {
97		r.t.Helper()
98		wt, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "land-test-wt-*")
99		if err != nil {
100			r.t.Fatal(err)
101		}
102		_ = os.RemoveAll(wt)
103		r.runGit("worktree", "add", "--detach", wt, baseSHA)
104		defer func() {
105			r.runGit("worktree", "remove", "--force", wt)
106			_ = os.RemoveAll(wt)
107		}()
108	
109		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(wt, path), []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
110			r.t.Fatal(err)
111		}
112		cmd := exec.Command("git", "-C", wt, "add", "-A")
113		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
114			r.t.Fatalf("git add: %s: %v", string(out), err)
115		}
116		cmd = exec.Command("git", "-C", wt, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.local", "commit", "-m", msg)
117		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
118			r.t.Fatalf("git commit: %s: %v", string(out), err)
119		}
120		cmd = exec.Command("git", "-C", wt, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

// TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields verifies that runtime evidence fields are
// populated in the JSON output. Exercises real git plumbing + the script
// harness so the fields are filled by the real runner, not a canned Result.
func TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()

	scrubEnv := func() []string {
		parent := os.Environ()
		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
		for _, kv := range parent {
			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
				continue
			}
			env = append(env, kv)
		}
		return env
	}
	runGit := func(args ...string) string {
		t.Helper()
		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
		cmd.Dir = workDir
		cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
	}

	runGit("init", "-b", "main")
	runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
	runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	runGit("add", ".")
	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")

	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:        "my-bead",
		Title:     "Test execute-bead evidence fields",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
	})
	runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
	baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")

	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
		"create-file evidence.txt evidence-content\n"+
			"commit feat: add evidence\n",
	), 0o644))

	f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})

	root := f.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
		"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
	require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
	res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)

	assert.Equal(t, "script", res.Harness)
	assert.Equal(t, directivePath, res.Model)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Tokens, "script harness reports no token usage")
	assert.Equal(t, 0.0, res.CostUSD, "script harness reports no cost")
	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
	assert.False(t, res.StartedAt.IsZero())
	assert.False(t, res.FinishedAt.IsZero())
	assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
	assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, "agent commit must produce a distinct ResultRev")
}
